action.reasoning: Observation: Custom get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth + renderer direct call validated successfully in inspect_render.py (correct rays_o/rays_d on CUDA, renderer SUCCESS with tensor output, relative az~185° computed from cameras). LPIPS stuck at 0.6121 from prior orbit rendering. Analysis: Default n_views=11 spherical orbit uses fixed azimuths starting at ~0° (per get_spherical_cameras source), mismatching dataset's per-target azimuths (large variance, input az not canonical); this caused all pose misalignment and poor metrics. Custom per-target rays with rel_az=(target_az-input_az)%360 + exact elev/dist + renderer call will produce pose-aligned novel views. TripoSR zero-shot remains strong (task-aligned, <1B params, no training). Decision: Port validated custom func + per-target loop + robust tensor-to-RGBA PIL handling (handles common output shapes like [1,3,H,W] or [H,W,3], 0-1 range, adds opaque alpha) into predict.py; add --num_objects for safe local val testing per workflow. Test on 5 val objs to confirm no crashes/saves correct RGBA 256x256 PNGs, then submit_eval for updated LPIPS (expect major drop) + time measurement. No fine-tuning yet.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/val_predictions.json --num_objects 5 && echo '=== Test completed successfully ===' && ls /tmp/val_predictions_renders
